title: Awesome Aunts and Uncles Get Their Day
story: Every July 26, families across America celebrate National Aunt and Uncle Day by showing extra love to these special relatives! Kids call, text, or visit their aunts and uncles to say thank you for all the fun times, cool gifts, and great advice. Many families post throwback photos on social media or plan special outings together. Some aunts and uncles get homemade cards or their favorite treats as surprises.
activity_question: How do kids typically celebrate their aunts and uncles on this day?
activity_choices: By calling, texting, or visiting them
By doing their homework for them
By teaching them new dance moves
activity_answer: By calling, texting, or visiting them
follow_up_question: What makes your aunt or uncle special to you?

title: Shake It Up with Coffee Power!
story: Coffee milkshakes get their own special day every July 26! This creamy treat combines the rich taste of coffee with the sweetness of ice cream and milk. People celebrate by visiting ice cream shops, diners, and cafes that serve these delicious drinks. Some families make their own at home using vanilla ice cream, cold coffee, and a blender. Coffee shops often create special flavors just for this day, like mocha chip or caramel coffee shakes. Even kids who don't drink regular coffee can enjoy these sweet, mild versions that taste more like dessert than grown-up coffee!
activity_question: What do people use to make coffee milkshakes at home?
activity_choices: Vanilla ice cream, cold coffee, and a blender
activity_answer: Vanilla ice cream, cold coffee, and a blender
bonus_fact: Some coffee milkshakes contain less caffeine than a regular soda!

title: Bagelfest: The Hole-y Grail of July
story: Every July 26, bagel lovers across America celebrate National Bagelfest Day by munching on their favorite round treats! People line up at bakeries for fresh-baked everything bagels, sesame seed classics, and wild flavors like blueberry or chocolate chip. Some families host bagel brunches with cream cheese, lox, and all the fixings. Coffee shops see their busiest bagel day of the year, and social media fills with photos of perfectly toasted bagels. Many people try making homemade bagels for the first time, discovering just how tricky it is to get that perfect chewy texture and shiny crust.
activity_question: What do many people try doing for the first time on National Bagelfest Day?
activity_choices: Eating bagels with a fork and knife
follow_up_question: What's the most unusual bagel flavor you'd be willing to try?

title: Go Big or Go Home Day!
story: Every July 26, people celebrate National All or Nothing Day by diving headfirst into their biggest dreams and wildest ideas! This holiday encourages everyone to stop playing it safe and go for broke. Some people finally ask their crush to hang out, while others attempt that crazy skateboard trick they've been practicing. Families might plan spontaneous road trips or try cooking elaborate recipes they've never attempted before. The whole point is to embrace bold choices and take meaningful risks on this special day each year.
activity_question: What does National All or Nothing Day encourage people to do?
activity_choices: Take meaningful risks and go for their dreams
Stay home and play video games all day
activity_answer: Take meaningful risks and go for their dreams
follow_up_question: What's one big dream you'd be brave enough to chase on All or Nothing Day?

title: Independence Day for Everyone!
story: Every July 26, people across America celebrate National Disability Independence Day! This special day honors the amazing strength and achievements of people with disabilities. Communities host parades, festivals, and fun activities that bring everyone together. Schools and libraries share stories about disability heroes who changed the world. Many people post inspiring messages on social media using special hashtags. It's a day to celebrate how everyone deserves equal chances to learn, work, and follow their dreams, no matter what challenges they face.
activity_question: What do communities often host on National Disability Independence Day?
follow_up_question: How can you help make your school or community more welcoming for everyone?

title: Breaking the Elevator Silence
story: Most people stand quietly in elevators, staring at the floor numbers or their phones. But on July 26, National Talk in an Elevator Day encourages everyone to break that awkward silence! People start conversations with strangers, compliment someone's shoes, or ask about their day. Some folks share fun facts or tell jokes during their short rides. It's amazing how a simple "How's your day going?" can turn a boring elevator trip into a friendly moment that brightens everyone's mood.
activity_question: What do most people usually do in elevators?
activity_choices: Stand quietly and stare at floor numbers
activity_answer: Stand quietly and stare at floor numbers
follow_up_question: What would you say to a stranger in an elevator to make them smile?

title: Custard's Last Stand: Vanilla Victory Day
story: Spoons everywhere celebrate on July 26 when National Vanilla Custard Day arrives! This creamy, dreamy dessert gets its own special day because vanilla custard is the perfect blend of smooth eggs, milk, sugar, and vanilla that creates pure magic. Bakeries showcase their best custard tarts, ice cream shops feature vanilla custard flavors, and home cooks whip up batches from scratch. Some people eat it warm, others prefer it chilled, but everyone agrees that this simple yet elegant treat deserves recognition on the 26th of this summer month.
activity_question: What ingredients combine to make vanilla custard special?
activity_choices: Cookies, frosting, sprinkles, and magic dust
bonus_fact: Custard was accidentally invented when a cook's pudding recipe went wonderfully wrong!
